title: DealJockey Phase 1 demo dashboard
description:
Create a simple HTML dashboard that exercises and demonstrates all DealJockey Phase 1 capabilities in one view.

## What it should show

A single-page HTML app (Flask route in ad_buyer_system) that demonstrates:

1. **Schema Status** — shows schema version, table counts, column list for deals table
2. **Deal CRUD** — form to create a deal with v2 fields, table showing all deals
3. **CSV Import** — file upload for CSV, shows import results (success/error counts, parsed deals)
4. **Manual Deal Entry** — form with validation, shows result
5. **Portfolio Inspection** — 
   - List view with filter dropdowns (status, media_type, deal_type, seller)
   - Search box
   - Summary statistics panel
   - Click-to-inspect detail view
6. **Event Log** — shows Phase 1 events (deal.imported, deal.template_created, portfolio.inspected, deal.manual_action_required)
7. **Agent Info** — shows DealJockey agent config (role, goal, backstory excerpt)

## Technical approach

- Single Flask blueprint with a few routes (HTML page + JSON API endpoints)
- Simple HTML/CSS (no React/npm — just Jinja2 templates or inline HTML)
- Uses DealStore with a demo SQLite database (not :memory: — persists across page refreshes)
- Pre-seed with sample data (10-15 example deals across different media types, sellers, statuses)
- Should work standalone: python -m ad_buyer.demo.dealjockey_dashboard or a Flask route

## Why

Replaces the need for terminal-based UAT. Aidan and reviewers can see everything working in a browser. Also serves as a living demo for the DealJockey capability.

Strategic plan: Section 9 Phase 1
Affected repo: ad_buyer_system
Depends on: all Phase 1 beads (all closed)

title: Campaign state machine
description:
Implement CampaignStatus enum with full state transitions in state_machine.py. States: DRAFT, PLANNING, BOOKING, READY, ACTIVE, COMPLETED, plus PAUSED, PACING_HOLD, CANCELED.

Strategic plan: ar-qt9, Section 6.6 (Campaign State Machine).
Gap from Section 10.4 row 2.
Phase 2 epic: buyer-an0.
Wave: Pre-wave prerequisite (blocks Wave 2).
Affected repo: ad_buyer_system

## States
- DRAFT → PLANNING (brief validated)
- PLANNING → BOOKING (plan approved)
- BOOKING → READY (all deals booked, creative validated and assigned)
- READY → ACTIVE (flight start or manual activation)
- ACTIVE → COMPLETED (flight end)
- ACTIVE → PAUSED (manual)
- ACTIVE → PACING_HOLD (pacing deviation)
- Any → CANCELED

## READY State
The READY state (per buyer-78z) is included here. buyer-78z tracks the specific requirement; this bead implements the full state machine including READY. buyer-78z is a dependency of this bead.

## File Location
models/state_machine.py (alongside existing BuyerCampaignStatus enum)

title: Human approval gates / event bus
description:
Configurable human approval gates at each campaign pipeline stage. Implements the approval_config pattern from D-3: each campaign brief specifies which stages require human review before proceeding.

Strategic plan: ar-qt9, UC-1 (use case: approval at each stage) and D-3 (configurable approval decision).
Phase 2 epic: buyer-an0.
Wave: Cross-cutting (after event types and state machine).
Affected repo: ad_buyer_system

## Approval Points
- Plan review: after plan generation, before deal booking
- Deal booking: after deals selected, before committing budget
- Creative assignment: after creative matched, before ad server push
- Campaign activation: after READY state, before ACTIVE

## Implementation
- approval_config in campaign brief JSON (default: require approval for plan + booking)
- Event bus integration: approval-required events trigger hold state
- Approval API: approve/reject endpoint per campaign per stage
- Auto-approve mode: skip gates when approval_config allows

## Dependencies
- Campaign event types (buyer-ppi)
- Campaign state machine (buyer-0u9)

title: Fix PROGRESS.md generation to read from database instead of stale JSONL
description:
PROGRESS.md generator (generate_progress.py) reads from issues.jsonl, but bd operations update the SQLite database. The JSONL is only updated when bd sync or bd export runs. This means PROGRESS.md can show stale data.

Symptoms observed:
- After updating bead titles with [LEGACY] prefix, PROGRESS.md still showed old titles
- After importing 46 beads, PROGRESS.md didn't reflect them until manual bd export
- Had to run 'bd export > issues.jsonl' manually before generate_progress.py would see current state

Options:
1. Change generate_progress.py to read from SQLite database directly (preferred — always current)
2. Add 'bd export' step before generate_progress.py in the GitHub Action
3. Hook generate_progress.py into bd's auto-flush mechanism

The GitHub Action that runs on push to main also needs to be checked — it may have the same stale-read issue.

title: Fix PROGRESS.md generator to handle tombstoned/compacted dependency references
description:
## Problem

When a bead is tombstoned or compacted (e.g., buyer-gf2), dependency references to it from other beads remain in issues.jsonl. The generate_progress.py script faithfully renders these stale dependencies in the Blockers column of PROGRESS.md, even when the referenced bead no longer exists and the dependent bead is already closed.

This caused buyer-5er (1I) and buyer-cjx (1J) to show buyer-gf2 as a blocker on the web page despite both being completed.

## Root Cause

generate_progress.py does not check whether a dependency target still exists or whether the dependent bead is already closed. It blindly renders all dependency records.

## Fix Options

1. **Generator fix**: generate_progress.py should skip dependencies where the target bead is tombstoned/missing, or where the source bead is already closed
2. **bd close fix**: When closing a bead, bd should clean up dependency records pointing to it from other beads
3. **bd compact fix**: When compacting/tombstoning a bead, remove or update dependency references in other beads
4. **Both**: Fix the generator for display correctness AND fix the lifecycle commands for data correctness

## Workaround Applied

Manually removed buyer-gf2 dependency entries from buyer-5er and buyer-cjx in issues.jsonl and regenerated PROGRESS.md (PR #59).

## Affected Repos

The generate_progress.py script exists in each repo's .beads/ directory. Fix should apply to all repos.

- Returns ranked comparison table: deal_id, path_description, base_cpm, estimated_fees, effective_cpm, fill_rate, hop_count, recommendation
- Handles cases where only one deal exists for an inventory (no comparison needed)
- Tests cover: comparison of 3 paths to same inventory, fee estimation from hop count, ranking by effective CPM, single-path scenario

## TDD Guidance
Write comparison tests FIRST: seed DB with 3 deals to same inventory at different paths, test effective CPM calculation, test ranking order. Then implement.

title: Implement deal duplication for new advertisers
description:

Process:
1. User specifies source deal(s) and target advertiser
2. Read source deal parameters from library
3. Create booking request for each deal, replacing advertiser/buyer identity
4. Book through internal modules (quote-then-book or from-template)
5. New deals added to portfolio with parent_deal_id linking to source

Adjustments: different advertiser (always), adjusted pricing (optional), modified targeting (optional), different flight dates (optional).

Strategic plan: Section 7.5, Section 10.3
Affected repo: ad_buyer_system
Depends on: template CRUD (buyer-dj5 / buyer-te6b.1.13), internal deal-booking modules (buyer-te6b.1.6)

title: Build deal deprecation analysis and execution
description:

Deprecation criteria (configurable):
- Zero delivery in N days (default: 90)
- Fill rate below threshold (default: 2%)

- Duplicate of a better-performing deal

Process: analyze -> produce deprecation report -> user reviews -> execute cancellations -> optionally archive.

title: 3D-Phase2: Mediaocean Lumina (Linear TV)
description: Integrate with Mediaocean Lumina for linear TV order management. Lumina is the linear TV buying and stewardship platform used by agencies for national and local TV. Integration enables: linear TV buy plan import, daypart/program-level order placement, makegood tracking and resolution, post-log reconciliation, and audience delivery verification (Nielsen/Comscore). The Linear TV buyer agent (1G) should use Lumina as its order management backbone. Supports TIP standard for electronic avails, orders, and invoicing with sellers.
